original-metadata.title: Ministerial Order BA 2024 06 - Adaptable Dwelling Units, Part 9 Seismic, and General Updates
original-metadata.description: Revision 6 changes include updates to make adaptable dwelling units easier to design and construct, offering greater flexibility that can reduce space requirements and costs. The changes also clarify seismic requirements for houses and small buildings (Part 9) to support builder understanding and compliance. Additional general updates are also included.
original-metadata.authority: BC Ministry of Housing - Building and Safety Standards Branch

1Where the building or part thereof is constructed in accordance with , a fire separation with a 2 h fire-resistance rating is required between the Group C and Group A, Division 2 major occupancies.
1Where the building or part thereof is constructed in accordance with , a fire separation with a 2 h fire-resistance rating is required between the Group D and Group A, Division 2 major occupancies.

amendment.description: Repeal Article 3.1.6.4 and substitute new Encapsulation of Mass Timber Elements

article.title: Encapsulation of Mass Timber Elements (See also .)

sentence.text:
Except as provided in ,
, and , the exposed surfaces of structural mass timber elements conforming to shall be protected from adjacent spaces in the building, including adjacent concealed spaces within wall, floor and roof assemblies, by a material or assembly of materials conforming to that provides an encapsulation rating that

clause.text: is not less than 50 min in a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or , or
conforms to the minimum values stated in for the applicable major occupancy and building height. (See .)

sentence.text: Except as provided in , the material or assembly of materials referred to in shall consist of

clause.text: gypsum board,
gypsum concrete,
noncombustible materials,
materials that conform to , or
any combination of the materials listed in .

sentence.text: Except as provided in , the exposed surfaces of mass timber beams, columns and arches within a suite or fire compartment in a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or or permitted by to have a 50 min encapsulation rating need not be protected in accordance with , provided

clause.text: their aggregate exposed surface area does not exceed 35% of the total wall area of the perimeter of the suite or fire compartment in which they are located, and
the flame-spread rating on any exposed surface is not more than 150. (See .)

sentence.text: Except as provided in , the exposed surfaces of mass timber walls within a suite in a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or or permitted by to have a 50 min encapsulation rating need not be protected in accordance with , provided

clause.text: all portions of the exposed surfaces

subclause.text: face the same direction, or
are separated by a horizontal distance of not less than 4.5 m, and

clause.text: the flame-spread rating on any exposed surface is not more than 150. (See .)

sentence.text: Except as provided in , the aggregate exposed surface area of mass timber elements within a suite permitted in shall not exceed 35% of the total wall area of the perimeter of the suite. (See .)

Except as provided by , the exposed surfaces of mass timber ceilings within a suite or fire compartment, other than an exit or public corridor, in a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or or required by to have a 50 min encapsulation rating need not be protected in accordance with , provided their aggregate surface area does not exceed

clause.text: 10% of the total ceiling area of the suite or fire compartment, where the flame-spread rating on any exposed surface is not more than 150, or
25% of the total ceiling area of the suite or fire compartment, where the flame-spread rating on any exposed surface of a mass timber wall or ceiling is not more than 75. (See .)

sentence.text: The exposed surfaces of mass timber ceilings within a suite in a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or or required by to have a 50 min encapsulation rating need not be protected in accordance with , provided

clause.text: the aggregate surface area of any exposed mass timber beams, columns and arches does not exceed 20% of the total wall area of the perimeter of the suite in which they are located,
all surfaces of mass timber walls are

subclause.text: protected in accordance with , or

mass timber walls that are not otherwise permitted to be exposed in accordance with are protected by a material or assembly of materials conforming to that provides an encapsulation rating of not less than 80 min, and

clause.text: the flame-spread rating on any exposed mass timber wall or ceiling is not more than 75. (See .)

sentence.text: Structural mass timber elements in a building or part of a building permitted by to have an encapsulation rating of 0 min need not be protected in accordance with , provided

clause.text: mass timber walls and ceilings within vertical service spaces, public corridors, and exits are protected from adjacent spaces with a material or assembly of materials conforming to that provides an encapsulation rating of not less than 25 min, and
concealed spaces are protected in conformance with . (See .)

sentence.text: In a building or part of a building required by to have a minimum encapsulation rating of 70 min, the upper side of a mass timber floor or roof assembly is permitted to be encapsulated by a material or assembly of materials conforming to that provides an encapsulation rating of 50 min.

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted Article 3.1.6.4 Encapsulation of Mass Timber Elements

amendment.description: Repeal Article 3.1.6.6 and substitute new Encapsulation Materials

sentence.text: Gypsum-concrete topping and concrete not less than 38 mm thick are deemed to have an encapsulation rating of 50 min when installed on the upper side of a mass timber floor or roof assembly.

One layer of Type X gypsum board conforming to , "Standard Specification for Gypsum Board," or , "Gypsum Board," not less than 12.7 mm thick, is deemed to have an encapsulation rating of 25 min when installed on a mass timber element in accordance with .

Two layers of Type X gypsum board conforming to , "Standard Specification for Gypsum Board," or , "Gypsum Board," each not less than 12.7 mm thick, are deemed to have an encapsulation rating of 50 min when installed on a mass timber element in accordance with .

Two layers of Type X gypsum board conforming to , "Standard Specification for Gypsum Board," or , "Gypsum Board," each not less than 15.9 mm thick, are deemed to have an encapsulation rating of 70 min when installed on a mass timber element in accordance with .

Three layers of Type X gypsum board conforming to , "Standard Specification for Gypsum Board," or , "Gypsum Board," each not less than 12.7 mm thick, are deemed to have an encapsulation rating of 80 min when installed on a mass timber element in accordance with .
Installation of the gypsum board described in shall be

clause.text: fastened with a minimum of two rows of screws in each layer

subclause.text: directly to the mass timber element with screws of sufficient length to penetrate not less than 20 mm into the mass timber element that are spaced not more than 400 mm o.c. and 20 mm to 38 mm from the boards' edges, or
to wood furring or resilient metal or steel furring channels not more than 25 mm thick spaced not more than 400 mm o.c. on the mass timber element,

clause.text: for multiple layer systems, installed with the joints in each layer staggered from those in the adjacent layer, and
installed in conformance with , "Standard Specification for Application and Finishing of Gypsum Board," except that, for multiple layer systems, their joints need not be taped and finished. (See .)

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted Article 3.1.6.6 Encapsulation Materials

amendment.description: Repeal Article 3.1.6.9 and substitute new Exterior Cladding

sentence.text:
Except as provided in
, cladding on an exterior wall assembly of a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction shall be

clause.text: noncombustible,
a material or combination of materials that satisfy the criteria of ,
except as provided in , a wall assembly that satisfies the criteria of , or
a combination of the cladding described in .

sentence.text:
Except as provided in
, cladding on an exterior wall assembly of a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction that is not more than 12 storeys in building height is permitted to consist of

clause.text: combustible cladding that

subclause.text: is not contiguous over more than 4 storeys,
represents not more than 10% of the cladding on each exterior wall of each storey,
is not more than 1.2 m in width,
has a flame-spread rating not more than 75 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction,
is separated from other portions of combustible cladding on adjacent storeys by a horizontal distance of not less than 2.4 m, and
is separated from other portions of combustible cladding by a horizontal distance of not less than 1.2 m,

clause.text: combustible cladding that

subclause.text: is not contiguous across adjacent storeys,
represents not more than 10% of the cladding on each exterior wall of each storey,
has a flame-spread rating not more than 75 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction, and
is separated from other portions of combustible cladding on adjacent storeys by a horizontal distance of not less than 2.4 m,

clause.text: combustible cladding representing up to 100% of the cladding on exterior walls of the first storey, provided all portions of the cladding can be directly accessed and are located not more than 15 m from a street or access route conforming to , measured horizontally from the face of the building, or
a combination of noncombustible cladding and the cladding described in and .

sentence.text: The permitted area of combustible cladding in or shall not exceed 5% of the cladding on each exterior wall of each storey where the time from receipt of notification of a fire by the fire department until the arrival of the first fire department vehicle at the building exceeds 10 min in 10% or more of all fire department calls to the building. (See .)

Except as provided in , cladding on an exterior wall assembly of a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction that is not more than 6 storeys in building height is permitted to consist of

clause.text: combustible cladding that

subclause.text: represents not more than 10% of the cladding on each exterior wall of each storey, and
has a flame-spread rating not more than 75 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction, or

clause.text: a combination of the cladding described in and the cladding described in and .

sentence.text: Where combustible cladding conforming to , or on an exterior wall of a fire compartment is exposed to combustible cladding conforming to , or on an exterior wall of the same fire compartment or of another fire compartment, and the planes of the two walls are parallel or at an angle less than 135Â° measured from the exterior of the building, the different portions of combustible cladding shall

clause.text: be separated by a horizontal distance of not less than 3 m, and
not be contiguous over more than 2 storeys.

sentence.text: Except as provided in , cladding on an exterior wall assembly of a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction and not more than 4 storeys in building height is permitted to consist of combustible material with a flame-spread rating not more than 75 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction. (See Note A-3.1.6.9.(1), (2), (4) and (6).)
An exterior wall assembly constructed in conformance with Section D-6 of Appendix D is deemed to satisfy the criteria of .
Except as provided in , where the limiting distance in or permits an area of unprotected openings of not more than 10% of the exposing building face, the construction requirements of shall be met.

A wall assembly conforming to that includes combustible cladding made of fire-retardant-treated wood shall be tested for fire exposure after the cladding has been subjected to the accelerated weathering test specified in , "Standard Practice for Accelerated Weathering of Fire-Retardant-Treated Wood for Fire Testing."

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted Article 3.1.6.9 Exterior Cladding

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.1.6.14.(2) to add references to (7) and (8)

content.text:
Except as provided in
, combustible interior wall finishes, other than foamed plastics, that are not more than 25 mm thick are permitted in a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction, provided they have a flame-spread rating not more than 150 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction.

revision.change-summary: Added references to Sentences 3.1.6.4.(7) and (8)

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.1.6.14.(3) to add references to (7) and (8)

content.text:
Except as provided in
, combustible interior ceiling finishes, other than foamed plastics, that are not more than 25 mm thick are permitted in a building or part of a building permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction, provided they have a flame-spread rating not more than 25 on any exposed surface, or any surface that would be exposed by cutting through the material in any direction, except that not more than 10% of the ceiling area within each fire compartment is permitted to have a flame-spread rating not more than 150. (See .)

revision.change-summary: Added references to Sentences 3.1.6.4.(7) and (8)

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.1.7.5.(1) by changing reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

content.text: Except as permitted by and by for mixed types of construction, all loadbearing walls, columns and arches in the storey immediately below a floor or roof assembly required to have a fire-resistance rating shall have a fire-resistance rating not less than that required for the supported floor or roof assembly.

revision.change-summary: Changed reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.1.11.5.(4) by adding reference to 3.2.2.93

content.text: Except for crawl spaces conforming to and except as provided in , in buildings or parts thereof conforming to , or , horizontal concealed spaces within a floor assembly or roof assembly of encapsulated mass timber construction shall be separated by construction conforming to into compartments that are

clause.text: not more than 600 m2 in area with no dimension more than 60 m, if the exposed construction materials within the space have a flame-spread rating not more than 25, and
not more than 300 m2 in area with no dimension more than 20 m, if the exposed construction materials within the space have a flame-spread rating more than 25. (See .)

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.1.15.2.(5) by adding reference to 3.2.2.93

content.text: Where buildings or parts thereof conforming to , , , or include non-contiguous roof assemblies at different elevations, the roof coverings referred to in are permitted to be evaluated separately to determine the roof covering classification required.

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Sentence 3.2.1.4.(1) with updated references including 3.2.2.93

content.text: Except as permitted by , , , , , , , , or , a floor assembly immediately above a basement shall be constructed as a fire separation having a fire-resistance rating conforming to the requirements of for a floor assembly, but not less than 45 min.

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted with updated references including 3.2.2.93.(3) and extended article range to 3.2.2.93

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.2.1.6.(1) by changing reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

content.text: The floor assembly of a mezzanine that is required to be considered as a storey in calculating the building height shall be constructed in conformance with the fire separation requirements for floor assemblies stated in .

revision.change-summary: Changed reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

amendment.description: Repeal Article 3.2.2.6 and substitute new Multiple Major Occupancies with additional sentence

sentence.text:
Except as permitted by , and
, in a building containing more than one major occupancy, the requirements of this Subsection for the most restricted major occupancy contained shall apply to the whole building.

In a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with , containing more than one major occupancy, the most restrictive encapsulation requirements of and for any major occupancy contained within a storey shall apply to the encapsulation required on the interior of a public corridor or exit within that storey.

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted Article 3.2.2.6 Multiple Major Occupancies with updated references and additional sentence for encapsulation requirements

amendment.description: Repeal Article 3.2.2.7 and substitute new Superimposed Major Occupancies with additional sentence and encapsulation requirements

sentence.text:
Except as provided in and
, in a building in which one major occupancy is located entirely above another major occupancy, the requirements in this Subsection for each portion of the building containing a major occupancy shall apply to that portion as if the entire building were of that major occupancy.

If one major occupancy is located above another major occupancy, the fire-resistance rating of the floor assembly between the major occupancies shall be determined on the basis of the requirements of this Subsection for the lower major occupancy. (See also )
In a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with , , if one major occupancy is located above another major occupancy,

clause.text: the most restrictive encapsulation requirements of and for any major occupancy contained within the building shall apply to the encapsulation required on the interior of vertical service spaces and exit stairs, and
the encapsulation requirements of and for a mass timber floor assembly between the major occupancies shall be determined on the basis of the requirements for

subclause.text: the upper major occupancy for the encapsulation of the upper surface of the mass timber floor assembly, and
the lower major occupancy for the encapsulation of the underside of the mass timber floor assembly.

revision.change-summary: Repealed and substituted Article 3.2.2.7 Superimposed Major Occupancies with updated references and additional sentence for encapsulation requirements

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.2.2.11.(1) by changing reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

content.text: Except as provided in , an exterior balcony shall be constructed in accordance with the type of construction required by , as applicable to the occupancy classification of the building.

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Article 3.2.2.18 - Automatic Sprinkler System

sentence.text: If a storey in a building or a floor area is required to have an automatic sprinkler system installed throughout in accordance with one or more of Articles 3.2.2.20. to 3.2.2.93. or Section 3.3., the automatic sprinkler system shall also be installed throughout all lower storeys in the building notwithstanding permission in Articles 3.2.2.20. to 3.2.2.93. to construct one or more of those storeys without installing automatic sprinkler protection. (See Note A-3.2.2.18.(2).)

amendment.description: Insert Article 3.2.2.93 - Encapsulated Mass Timber Construction, Various Occupancies, Heights and Areas, Sprinklered after Article 3.2.2.92

content.title: Encapsulated Mass Timber Construction, Various Occupancies, Heights and Areas, Sprinklered

sentence.text: A building that is classified as Group A, Division 2, Group B, Division 3, Group C, Group D, Group E, or Group F, Division 2 or 3, is permitted to conform to Sentence (2) provided

clause.text: except as permitted by Sentences 3.2.2.7.(1) and 3.2.2.18.(2), the building is sprinklered,
the building has a building height not exceeding the number of storeys shown in Table 3.2.2.93. for the applicable major occupancy and minimum encapsulation rating,

the building has a maximum height that conforms to the value shown in Table 3.2.2.93. for the applicable major occupancy and minimum encapsulation rating that is measured between the floor of the first storey and the uppermost floor level that does not serve a rooftop enclosure for elevator machinery, a stairway or a service room used only for service to the building,
the building has a maximum building area that conforms to the value shown in Table 3.2.2.93. for the applicable major occupancy, and

except as provided in Sentences 3.1.6.3.(4) and 3.1.6.7.(1) and Article 3.1.6.4., the encapsulation rating conforms to the value shown in Table 3.2.2.93. for the applicable major occupancy and maximum building height.

table.title: Table 3.2.2.93. Encapsulated Mass Timber Construction Requirements

table-notes.note: (1) See Sentences (5) to (7) and Articles 3.2.2.4. to 3.2.2.8. for information pertaining to multiple major occupancies.
(2) Linear interpolation is not permitted in using Table 3.2.2.93.

sentence.text: Except as provided in Article 3.2.2.16., the building referred to in Sentence (1) is permitted to be of encapsulated mass timber construction or noncombustible construction, used singly or in combination, and

clause.text: except as provided in Sentence (3), floor assemblies shall be fire separations with a fire-resistance rating not less than 2 hours,
mezzanines shall have a fire-resistance rating not less than 1 hour, and
loadbearing walls, columns and arches shall have a fire-resistance rating not less than that required for the supported assembly.

sentence.text: In a building classified as a Group C major occupancy that contains dwelling units that have more than one storey, subject to the requirements of Sentence 3.3.4.2.(3), the floor assemblies, including floors over basements, that are entirely contained within these dwelling units shall have a fire-resistance rating not less than 1 h, but need not be constructed as fire separations.

In buildings referred to in Sentence (1) where the roof assembly of an exit stairway or vertical service space used as an elevator hoistway has a height greater than 55 m measured from grade to the highest point of the roof assembly, the enclosure for the stairway or hoistway shall be of encapsulated mass timber construction or constructed of concrete.

Group E major occupancies and storage garages located in a building or part of a building classified as a Group A, Division 2, Group C, or Group D major occupancy within the scope of this Article are permitted to be constructed in accordance with this Article and the corresponding Group A, Division 2, Group C, or Group D major occupancy requirements contained in Table 3.2.2.93., provided

clause.text: the Group E major occupancy is located below the third storey, and
the storage garage is located below the fifth storey (see also Article 4.4.2.1.).

sentence.text: Group A, Division 2 major occupancies located in a building or part of a building classified as a Group C or Group D major occupancy within the scope of this Article are permitted to be constructed in accordance with this Article and the corresponding Group C or Group D major occupancy requirements contained in Table 3.2.2.93., provided they are located below the fourth storey. (See Note A-3.2.2.48.(4), 3.2.2.57.(3) and 3.2.2.93.(5) to (7).)

Group F, Division 2 and 3 major occupancies located in a building or part of a building classified as a Group D major occupancy within the scope of this Article are permitted to be constructed in accordance with this Article and the corresponding Group D major occupancy requirements contained in Table 3.2.2.93., provided they are located below the third storey. (See Note A-3.2.2.48.(4), 3.2.2.57.(3) and 3.2.2.93.(5) to (7).)

content.text: Structural members, including beams, columns and arches, that are placed wholly or partly outside the exterior face of a building and are less than 3 m from the property line or the centre line of a public thoroughfare shall be protected from exterior fire exposure by fire protection having a fire-resistance rating not less than that required for their protection from interior fire exposure, as stated in .

content.text: Except as required by , this Subsection applies to a building or part of a building constructed in conformance with or in which the floor level of the highest storey is more than 18 m above grade.

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 3.3.3.7.(4) by changing reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

content.text: A contained use area, in a building for which do not require the installation of an automatic sprinkler system, is not required to be sprinklered as required by provided

clause.text: the building is designed so that during a period of 2 h after the start of a fire in the contained use area other fire compartments will not contain more than 1% by volume of contaminated air from the contained use area,
the contained use area does not exceed 500 m2 in building area,
the contained use area does not exceed one storey in building height, and
the contained use area is separated from the remainder of the building by a fire separation with a fire-resistance rating of not less than 2 h.

revision.change-summary: Changed reference from 3.2.2.92 to 3.2.2.93

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.1.6.4.(1) - Encapsulation of Mass Timber Elements

content.title: Encapsulation of Mass Timber Elements

revision.change-summary: Updated to reference Sentences 3.1.6.4.(3) to (8) instead of (3) to (6), and updated Article references

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.1.6.4.(3) to (6) - Fire-Resistance Rating of Mass Timber with Exposed Surfaces

content.title: Fire-Resistance Rating of Mass Timber with Exposed Surfaces
content.paragraph: Portions of mass timber elements required to have a fire-resistance rating are permitted to be exposed in accordance with the permissions stated in ; however, it is important to note that applying those permissions does not waive the requirement for these elements to have a fire-resistance rating. In the calculation of the total wall area of the perimeter of a suite or fire compartment in , and , the area of any wall openings, such as doors or windows, is included.

revision.change-summary: Updated note number to (3) to (8), updated sentence references to include calculation details for wall area in Sentences (3), (5) and (7)

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.1.6.4.(4) - Exposed Surfaces of Mass Timber Walls

content.title: Exposed Surfaces of Mass Timber Walls
content.paragraph: The primary objective of encapsulating mass timber elements is to limit the probability that these elements will significantly contribute to fire spread and fire duration in the event of a fire. Since thick wood members require a source of imposed heat flux to burn, the stipulation in that any portions of the exposed surfaces of different mass timber walls within a suite either face the same direction or have a minimum horizontal distance between one another. If the sprinkler system fails to operate or to control the fire, this directional orientation or minimum distance is intended to avoid or reduce the potential for re-radiation between portions of burning mass timber surfaces on different walls, and particularly those that either face or are in close proximity to one another, which could sustain flaming combustion into the decay phase of a fire. Additionally, if the sprinkler system fails to operate or to control the fire, the maximum percentages of exposed surface areas and maximum flame-spread ratings stated in are intended to be insufficient to sustain a ventilation-controlled fire that might provide the radiation required to sustain flaming combustion into the decay phase of a fire.

revision.change-summary: Updated to reference Clause 3.1.6.4.(4)(a) and expanded explanation about directional orientation and minimum distance requirements

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.1.6.6. - Encapsulation Materials

content.title: Encapsulation Materials
content.paragraph: Research has been conducted on different types of encapsulation materials, such as gypsum board, gypsum concrete and cement board. The results of tests using an intermediate-scale furnace and of cone calorimeter tests indicate that a combustible timber element protected with a 38 mm thick layer of gypsum-concrete topping or with one (25 min), two (50 min) or three (80 min) layers of 12.7 mm Type X gypsum board or two layers (70 min) of 15.9 mm Type X gypsum board will not ignite or contribute significant heat to a fire until the time at which average temperatures of 325°C to 380°C are attained at the interface between the encapsulation material or assembly of materials and the combustible substrate. These temperatures are consistent with the ignition temperatures of wood-based materials.

revision.change-summary: Updated layer specifications to include specific timing (25 min, 50 min, 80 min, 70 min) and board thickness details (12.7 mm and 15.9 mm Type X gypsum board)

amendment.description: Amend Note A-3.1.6.6.(2) by changing note number from A-3.1.6.6.(2) to A-3.1.6.6.(6)

content.title: Protection of Gypsum Board from Foot Traffic
content.paragraph: Where gypsum board is used as the encapsulation material on the top of a mass timber floor assembly, it should be protected from physical impact arising from normal pedestrian traffic that could damage it and possibly compromise its encapsulation rating.

revision.change-summary: Changed note number from A-3.1.6.6.(2) to A-3.1.6.6.(6)

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.1.6.9.(1) and (2) - Exterior Cladding

content.title: Exterior Cladding
content.paragraph: The requirements in , and are intended to reduce the potential for fire spread on the exterior cladding of buildings of encapsulated mass timber construction through the use of noncombustible finishes on the exterior of the wall assembly or the use of a cladding/wall assembly that has been proven to resist flame propagation as a function of increasing building height, including provisions to allow 100% combustible cladding where the height does not exceed 4 storeys. These cladding/wall assembly combinations can be used as infill or panel-type walls between structural elements, or attached directly to a loadbearing structural system. Note that the requirements in do not supersede the provisions in regarding spatial separation and exposure protection.

revision.change-summary: Updated note number to include (4) and (6); added references to Sentences (4) and (6); added provision about 100% combustible cladding for buildings up to 4 storeys

amendment.description: Amend Note A-3.1.11.5.(3) and (4) by adding reference to 3.2.2.93

content.title: Fire Blocks in Concealed Spaces
content.paragraph: To reduce the risk of fire spread in combustible concealed spaces within the types of buildings referred to in , fire blocking is required regardless of whether the horizontal concealed space is protected by sprinklers or not, unless the space is filled with noncombustible insulation so that any air gap at the top of the insulation is very small. (See also for roof venting.)

A 5- or 6-storey building constructed in accordance with and buildings constructed in accordance with
or are required to be sprinklered in accordance with (see ). generally requires sprinklering of any concealed spaces of combustible construction or combustible contents in buildings equipped throughout with an automatic sprinkler system.

revision.change-summary: Added reference to Article 3.2.2.93 (changed "or 3.2.2.60." to ", 3.2.2.60. or 3.2.2.93.")

amendment.description: Repeal and substitute Note A-3.2.2.48.(4) and 3.2.2.57.(3) - Occupancy Combinations in Buildings of Mixed Construction

content.title: Occupancy Combinations in Buildings of Mixed Construction
content.paragraph: Buildings conforming to the building height and area limits and the other fire protection requirements of or may be entirely constructed of encapsulated mass timber construction and incorporate the occupancies specifically permitted by or to : e.g., Group A, Division 2 major occupancies on the first to third storeys, Group E major occupancies on the first and second storeys, and a parking garage on the first to fourth storeys.

Alternatively, the requirements of for superimposed major occupancies can be applied, resulting in buildings of mixed construction conforming to the building height and area limits for encapsulated mass timber construction and in which the lower storeys are of noncombustible construction and the upper storeys are of encapsulated mass timber construction. For example, a Group A, Division 2 or Group B, Division 3 major occupancy could be located on the first 4 storeys of a 12-storey Group C building constructed in accordance with , as long as these first 4 storeys were constructed of noncombustible construction in accordance with or , as applicable. (See also and .)

revision.change-summary: Updated note number to include 3.2.2.93.(5) to (7); added references to Article 3.2.2.93 and Sentences 3.2.2.93.(5) to (7)

amendment.description: Insert new Note A-3.2.2.93.(1) and Table 3.2.2.93. - Occupancy Combinations in Buildings of Mixed Encapsulation Ratings

content.title: Occupancy Combinations in Buildings of Mixed Encapsulation Ratings
content.paragraph: Buildings conforming to the building height and minimum encapsulation rating requirements and the other fire protection requirements of may be entirely constructed of encapsulated mass timber construction and incorporate the multiple major occupancies otherwise permitted by to . This would also include permitting mixing of major occupancies that require different levels of encapsulation for structural mass timber elements in accordance with .

amendment.description: Add Subsection 3.2.10. - Requirements for Residential Buildings with a Single Exit

content.title: Requirements for Residential Buildings with a Single Exit

sentence.text: Except as provided in to , this Subsection applies to buildings of only residential occupancy that

clause.text: are not more than six storeys in building height,

have a height not more than 18 m measured between the floor of the first storey and the uppermost floor level, excluding any floor level within a rooftop enclosure that is not considered as a storey in calculating building height in accordance with ,
contain not more than four dwelling units on each floor,
does not exceed an occupant load of 24 persons per floor, and
have a travel distance from

subclause.text: any part of the floor area to an exit not more than 25 m, and
each dwelling unit to an exit of not more than 6 m.

sentence.text: This Subsection does not apply to floor areas permitted by to be served by one exit.
This Subsection does not apply to detached houses, semi-detached houses, houses with a secondary suite, duplexes, triplexes, townhouses or row houses.
Residential buildings with a single exit are not permitted for

clause.text: hotels, motels, dormitories or lodging houses,
residential clubs, colleges or schools,
monasteries,
seniors' residences, or
care facilities accepted for residential use pursuant to provincial legislation.

sentence.text: This Subsection, in accordance with the application of this Article and in conformance with the provisions of this Subsection and all other applicable provisions of this Code, may be used as an alternate to the requirements for a second exit in .

Notwithstanding the permissions in to , a building to which this Subsection applies shall be sprinklered throughout with an automatic sprinkler system designed, constructed, installed and tested in conformance with , "Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems." (See .) (See .)
All balconies and decks shall be sprinklered in accordance with . (See .)

The exit facility shall be constructed either of noncombustible construction or, notwithstanding the application of , with finishes that do not exceed the maximum flame-spread rating and maximum smoke developed classification for exit stairways in , and shall

clause.text: be separated from all other spaces in the building by a fire separation having a fire-resistance rating not less than 2 h,
be not less than 1 500 mm wide,
discharge directly to the exterior of the building without passing through a lobby,
be designed to limit the probability of storage or the accumulation of material, and
have signs posted in conspicuous locations near each landing to indicate that storage is not permitted.

sentence.text: The discharge from the exit stair described in shall be located not less than 3 m and not more than 15 m from the closest portion of the access route required for fire department use, measured horizontally from the face of the building. (See .)
Except as permitted by and notwithstanding the scope of , a building to which this Subsection applies that is greater than four storeys in building height shall be designed in accordance with .
Fans required to limit smoke movement by shall be provided with an emergency power supply capable of operating under full load for not less than 2 h provided by an emergency generator. (See also .)

Pressurization of the stair shaft described in is not required if each doorway from the public corridor serving the exit facility is protected with a vestibule on the public corridor side of the doorway

clause.text: consisting of a closure in the public corridor

subclause.text: equipped with electromagnetic hold-open devices that release upon activation of the fire alarm system, and
that has a fire-protection rating not less than a 45 min, and

clause.text: that forms a space that is separated from the remainder of the building by a fire separation with a fire-resistance rating not less than 45 min

subclause.text: that contains no suite entry doors, and
with the distance from doorway to the exit facility and the closure not less than 1 800 mm long and a width of the path between doorways not less than 1 500 mm.

sentence.text: Elevator hoistways shall not be designed as a means of venting.
The systems for control of smoke movement required by shall be tested to ensure satisfactory operation. (See and .)

article.title: Doors in Public Corridors and Suite Entry Doors

sentence.text: All doors along public corridors and all suite entry doors shall be equipped with electromagnetic hold-open devices designed to release upon activation of the fire alarm system in accordance with .

Hold-open devices on suite entry doors shall not require a force applied to the door or release device of more than 22 N to allow the occupant to manually release the door from the hold-open position to allow it to close.
Suite entry doors shall have a fire-protection rating of not less than 45 min.

amendment.description: Insert Note A-3.2.10 - Fire Protection and Firefighting Assumptions for Residential Buildings with a Single Exit

content.title: Fire Protection and Firefighting Assumptions for Residential Buildings with a Single Exit.
content.paragraph: The firefighting assumptions discussed in Note A-3 raise circumstances where additional firefighting capabilities and additional building protection measures may be required. The provisions in Subsection 3.2.10. for residential buildings with a single exit were developed with the expectation of a high level of capacity of the local fire department for fire prevention activities, fire suppression activities, the ability of the fire department to assist in egress of occupants in the event of an emergency, as well as a secure water supply for the function of the automatic sprinkler system and the manual fire suppression activities of a responding fire department.

Maintenance of fire safety systems is essential for all buildings but becomes increasingly important when redundancies, such as a second and separate exit, are reduced or eliminated. The provisions for residential buildings with single exit stairs were developed with the expectation that fire officials routinely enforce the British Columbia Fire Code to ensure that the inspection intervals specified within are carried out, and that follow up inspections resulting from observed non-compliances occur at expedited intervals. Building owners and managers are required to be actively involved in carrying out the maintenance and upkeep of the building's fire safety systems. Residential buildings with a single exit should not be located in jurisdictions where a high level of fire prevention oversight cannot be maintained.

The provisions for residential buildings with single exit stairs were developed with the expectation that a permanent, paid fire department is available to respond to a fire event twenty-four hours a day, every day. Firefighting personnel are expected to have a high level of training and the fire department is expected to be suitably equipped to provide fire suppression activities appropriate to the buildings and developments in their jurisdiction within tight response times. Residential buildings with a single exit should not be located in jurisdictions where a high level of fire suppression activities cannot be maintained.

The premise of egress in the British Columbia Building Code is that occupants of residential buildings receive early notification of a fire event, and occupants are able to respond unassisted in such an event. Early notification however does not guarantee occupant self-evacuation and when redundancies, such as a second and separate exit, are reduced or eliminated, occupant response options are likewise reduced. The provisions for residential buildings with single exit stairs were developed with the expectation that the local fire department has a service level that can coordinate and assist occupants with moving to a safe place in a fire event. Building owners and managers must be actively involved in fire safety planning in conjunction with the local fire department. Residential buildings with a single exit should not be located in jurisdictions where a service level that includes search and rescue by firefighting personnel cannot be maintained.

The British Columbia Building Code requires an adequate water supply for firefighting be readily available and of sufficient volume and pressure to enable emergency response personnel to control fire growth so as to enable the safe evacuation of occupants and the conduct of search and rescue operations, prevent the fire from spreading to adjacent buildings, and provide a limited measure of property protection. As for any water-based fire protection systems or suppression activities, the water supply serving the building needs to be dependable over the life of the building through seasonal droughts and other eventualities. The provisions for residential buildings with single exit stairs were developed with the expectation of a highly dependable water supply would be available during construction and throughout the lifecycle of the building. Redundancies such as secondary or back up water supplies should to be considered to limit the probability that the water supply may become insufficient for an extended or even temporary period.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10 explaining fire protection and firefighting assumptions for residential buildings with single exit stairs

content.title: Residential Buildings Not Permitted.
content.paragraph: The residential uses described in Sentence 3.2.10.1.(4) include short term as well as long term accommodations where Subsection 3.2.10. is not permitted to apply. For example, longer-term hotels and rooming houses sometimes referred to as single room occupancy hotels or single room accommodations with or without private bathrooms or kitchens are not permitted to apply Subsection 3.2.10.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10.1.(4) clarifying residential buildings not permitted under Subsection 3.2.10

content.title: Automatic Sprinkler Systems.
content.paragraph: Automatic sprinkler systems serving a building to which Subsection 3.2.10. applies must be sprinklered in accordance with NFPA 13, "Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems," regardless of the permissions for other buildings to follow the NFPA 13R, "Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems in low-Rise Residential Occupancies," or the NFPA 13D, "Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems in One- and Two-Family Dwellings and Manufactured Homes," standards. All balconies and decks regardless of depth must be sprinklered.
The requirement for sprinklers conforming to NFPA 13, "Standard for the Installation of Sprinkler Systems," means that a fire alarm system described in Subsection 3.2.4. is also required.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10.2.(1) and (2) regarding automatic sprinkler system requirements for buildings under Subsection 3.2.10

content.title: Exit Facility.
content.paragraph: The single exit facility serving a building to which Subsection 3.2.10. applies shall either be of noncombustible construction or shall be finished as an exit stair serving an unsprinklered high building described in Subsection 3.2.6. Should a fire event occur, protection of the exit stairs must not solely rely on the automatic sprinkler system.

A 1 500 mm wide exit facility facilitates the movement and passing of people for regular use as well as in an emergency. The design of intermediate handrails, such as for widths exceeding 1 700 mm, should consider the movement of emergency personnel including their equipment on each side. The design of the exit facility must also consider misuse and proactively deter the storage or collection of materials so as not to obstruct passage and to minimize fuel load. For example, excess landing areas and alcoves in all exits (not limited to residential buildings with single exit stairs) must be carefully designed to deter any activities other than exiting and accessing floor areas as per Sentence 3.4.4.4.(6). Void spaces underneath stairs should be avoided. Landings must have signage to remind occupants that storage is not permitted in exit facilities.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10.2.(3) regarding exit facility design and construction requirements

content.title: Exit Facility Discharge.
content.paragraph: Fire department access to buildings to which Subsection 3.2.10. applies must be provided to the principal entrance as well as to the access point most directly connected to the exit facility. As such, the exit facility discharge must be located to coordinate with the fire department access route described in Subsection 3.2.5.

Designers should consider locating the exit facility discharge in close proximity to the principal entrance for the benefit of coordinating multiple access points with the fire department access route but also to avoid unintended use of the exit facility for package delivery or other material drop-off. There should be clear distinction between the principal building entrance (likely connecting to a lobby and elevator) and the exit facility discharge so that activities such as deliveries can be intuitively completed in the safe and intended manner. Locating the exit facility discharge in close proximity to the principal building entrance also reduces the potential that an access point is located in an isolated location of the building which could increase building and occupant security concerns.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10.2.(4) regarding exit facility discharge location and coordination with fire department access

content.title: Suite Entry Doors.
content.paragraph: Sentence 3.2.10.4.(3) supersedes the permission in Article 3.1.8.12. to reduce the fire-protection rating of closures. Suite entry doors with less than a 45 min fire-protection rating are not permitted in buildings to which Subsection 3.2.10. applies, nor would these doors be permitted any undercuts such as described in Article 9.32.3.10.

revision.change-summary: Added Note A-3.2.10.4.(3) regarding suite entry door fire-protection rating requirements

amendment.description: Add Sentence 3.8.5.1.(3) requiring one in five dwelling units to be adaptable

content.text: 3) At least one for every five or part thereof of dwelling units described in shall conform to this Subsection. (See .)

revision.change-summary: Added new sentence requiring one in five dwelling units to be adaptable

amendment.description: Add Application Note A-3.8.5.1.(3) explaining adaptable dwelling unit calculation

content.title: Application to Dwelling Units
content.paragraph: The Building Code requires that for every dwelling unit meeting the criteria described in that one for every five units be designed as an adaptable dwelling unit. To calculate, the designer shall take the number of units that meet the criteria of and then design not less than 20 percent of those units to be adaptable. "Or part thereof" means, for example, that if there are two units that meet the criteria of that one of them must be designed to be adaptable and that if there are six units that meet the criteria of that two of them must be designed to be adaptable.

revision.change-summary: Added new application note explaining adaptable dwelling unit calculation requirements

content.entry: Mechanical and Plumbing Systems Part 6 and Part 7

Existing mechanical and plumbing systems in buildings are not required to fully comply to the requirements of Parts 6 or Part 7 provided: (a) it is not an unsafe condition and (b) it is acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction.

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 5 by striking out italic 'Ramps' and substituting regular 'Ramps', and by striking out italic 'ramp' and substituting regular 'ramp' throughout Item 5

content.entry:
Openings Near Unenclosed Exterior Exit Stairs and Ramps
Unprotected openings in exterior walls that are within 3 m horizontally and less than 10 m below or less than 5 m above an unenclosed exterior exit stair or ramp of a house with a secondary suite shall be protected where the unenclosed exterior exit stair or ramp provides the only means of egress from a suite and is exposed to fire from unprotected openings in the exterior walls of another dwelling unit, ancillary space or common space.
Protection of the unprotected openings as described in is not required when all smoke alarms within a house with a secondary suite are of photo-electric type and interconnected as described in .

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 5 by striking out italic "Ramps" and substituting regular "Ramps", and by striking out italic "ramp" and substituting regular "ramp" throughout Item 5

amendment.description: Amend Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 7 by striking out 'Fire-Resistance and Fire-Protection Ratings' and substituting 'Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings'

content.entry:
Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings
In a house with a secondary suite, where a minimum fire-resistance rating of 30 min is permitted, it is permitted to use wood-frame construction where stud and joist spaces are filled with absorptive material,resilient metal channel spaced 400 or 600 mm o.c. is on one side and not less than 12.7 mm thick gypsum board is installed on ceilings and on both sides of walls.

Adding resilient metal channel spaced 400 or 600 mm o.c. and an additional layer of not less than 12.7 mm gypsum board to one side of an existing finished wall assembly that has not less than 12.7 mm gypsum board on each side or an existing finished floor-ceiling assembly that has not less than 12.7 mm gypsum on the ceiling side is permitted to be used where a 30 min fire-resistance rating is required.

revision.change-summary: Amended Table 1.1.1.1.(6), Item 7 by striking out "Fire-Resistance and Fire-Protection Ratings" and substituting "Fire-Resistance Ratings and Fire-Protection Ratings"

when provided, a closet with

subclause.text: a clear opening width of not less than 900 mm, and

a clear floor space, that need not be separate from the turning areas required in , of not less than 1 700 mm in diameter or 1 700 mm by 1 500 mm on at least one side of the closet. (See Note A-3.8.5.6.(1).)

sentence.text: Though the Code doesn't regulate bed furniture or sizes or styles such as bunkbeds, designs should allow for a bed to be located to accommodate transfer from the turning area to either side of the bed, such as by flipping the orientation of the bed, as well as for functional maneuvering space in the room.
A built-in closet is not required by this Code.

amendment.description: Amend Note A-9.4.2.5. Seismic Design Parameter paragraph by striking out 'Sentence 4.1.8.4.(6)' and substituting 'Sentence 4.1.8.4.(3)'

content.text: The seismic design parameter, Smax, is used as a trigger for the application of seismic design provisions in Part 9. It was derived by considering the upper limit on the minimum lateral earthquake force, V, as specified in , and is taken as the larger of 2/3 S(0.2) and S(0.5), with S(0.2) and S(0.5) calculated in accordance with .

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 9.37.1.2.(4) by striking out 'CO2e' and substituting 'CO₂e'

content.text: For the purposes of this Section, the symbol "CO2e" shall mean carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide equivalent, with carbon dioxide equivalent meaning one or more greenhouse gases with a global warming potential equivalent to carbon dioxide.

revision.change-summary: Struck out "CO2e" and substituted "CO₂e"

amendment.description: Amend Note A-9.4.2.5.(2) Determination of Site Class paragraph by striking out 'Article 4.1.8.4.' and substituting 'Sentence 4.1.8.4.(3)'

content.text: To benefit from a refined, and possibly less conservative, value of Smax, the Site Class can be determined on the basis of the ground profile at the site in accordance with . Determination of the Site Class will require the involvement of a suitably qualified and experienced professional engineer.

revision.change-summary: Amended reference from Article 4.1.8.4. to Sentence 4.1.8.4.(3)

amendment.description: Amend Sentence 9.37.1.2.(6) by striking out 'kgCO2e' and substituting 'kgCO₂e'

content.text: For the purposes of this Section, the term "total greenhouse gases" shall mean the total greenhouse gas emissions, expressed as kgCO2e/year, associated with the energy used by the building's systems.

amendment.description: Amend Notes to Part 9 Housing and Small Buildings, A-9.32.4.1. Naturally Aspirating Fuel-Fired Vented Appliance (NAFFVA) by striking out Tables A-9.32.4.1.(1)A and A-9.32.4.1.(1)B in their entirety, and substituting new tables

content.title: Naturally Aspirating Fuel-Fired Vented Appliance (NAFFVA)
content.paragraph: NAFFVA, typically appliances with draft hoods, are subject to back drafting when a negative pressure condition occurs in the dwelling. The following tables describe the conditions under which Sentence 9.32.4.1.(1) applies:

table.title: Table A-9.32.4.1.(1)A Vent Safety - Natural Gas and Propane

table-notes.note: (1) Any power vented appliance with pressurized vent (1 pipe) or sealed combustion (2 pipe) or direct vent appliance (fireplace, heater or HWT) are non-NAFFVA.
(2) Thermal buoyancy chimneys must be within the heated envelope of the house to provide acceptable venting performance.

(3) This category applies only to (a) mid-efficient forced air furnaces equipped with induced draft fans and exhaust proving switch, and (b) boilers equipped with induced draft fans and exhaust proving switch.
(4) Mid-efficient (draft induced) appliances are considered NAFFVA with the exception of a boiler or HWT located in an air-barriered room.

(5) Mechanical room must be air-barriered from remainder of house with no access from within house. Room must be lined with panel products with sealed joints and all pipe and wire penetrations sealed. Effectively, the room must be finished before equipment is installed and holes drilled for pipes and wires. This option is not available for forced air furnaces as it is not possible to effectively seal the ducts.

table.title: Table A-9.32.4.1.(1)B Vent Safety - Oil and Solid Fuel

row.entry: F/A Furnace Boiler HWT Fireplace Heat Stove

table-notes.note: (1) Thermal buoyancy chimneys must be within the heated envelope of the house to provide acceptable venting performance.

(2) Sealed combustion kits can be added to oil-fired appliances but they switch to interior combustion air if intake is blocked and rely on barometrically dampered thermal buoyancy chimneys so they are considered NAFFVA.
(3) Oil-fired HWT, boilers and furnaces equipped with blocked vent switches.

(4) Mechanical room must be air-barriered from remainder of house with no access from within house. Room must be lined with panel products with sealed joints and all pipe and wire penetrations sealed. Effectively, the room must be finished before equipment is installed and holes drilled for pipes and wires. This option is not available for forced air furnaces as it is not possible to effectively seal the ducts.
(5) Wood-burning appliances certified for use in mobile homes and installed to mobile home installation standards are considered non-NAFFVA and Sentence 9.32.4.1.(1) does not apply to them.

revision.change-summary: Replaced Tables A-9.32.4.1.(1)A and A-9.32.4.1.(1)B in their entirety with updated tables and notes

amendment.description: Amend Notes to Part 9 Housing and Small Buildings, A-9.36.1.3.(6) Exemptions (numbering) by striking out '9.36.1.3.(6)' and substituting '9.36.1.3.(5)'

content.title: Exemptions
content.paragraph: Examples of buildings and spaces that are exempted from the requirements of include seasonally heated buildings, storage and parking garages, small service buildings or service rooms and unconditioned spaces in buildings and unconditioned buildings such as storage warehouses. However, note that, where a building envelope assembly of an exempted building is adjacent to a conditioned space, this assembly must meet the requirements of .

amendment.description: Amend Appendix C, Climatic and Seismic Information for Building Design in Canada, by striking out 'for the 2010 edition of the Code' in the ninth paragraph under 'Wind Effects'

revision.content: As a result of the updating procedure, the 1-in-50 reference wind velocity pressures remained unchanged for most of the locations listed in . However, the reference wind velocity pressures for some locations were revised to reflect the availability of additional wind data. The reference wind velocity pressures for locations in British Columbia were revised to reflect the availability of additional wind data and the use of a different extreme value distribution.
revision.change-summary: Struck out "for the 2010 edition of the Code"
